task: Completed pre-launch safety audits on session management core (45 passing tests, checkpoint/guard/hook systems verified), identified critical packaging gap (brew Formula ships wrong package), documented roadmap findings, discovered and rotated leaked webhook credential, implemented dangerous-permission task approval gate with lifecycle signal-based companion session triggering, and added comprehensive test coverage for permission gate and task approval workflows.
discussion_summary: Session ran two autonomous audits in parallel: safety-gate verification (unbuilt gates, race conditions) and test-suite health check (packaging readiness). Core session-management infrastructure (hooks, checkpoint, guard, state sync) is production-ready with full test coverage. Critical blocker identified: homebrew Formula did not install the full askr/ package or create bin/askr entry point — now fixed to ship full package with proper entry point and requirements.txt deps. Discovered Discord webhook credential leaked in git history (rotated, requires git-filter-repo scrubbing). Completed dangerous-permission task approval gate (Phase 5 roadmap item): wired permission detection into session_start.py, added _peek_task_queue/_consume_approval_flag/_notify_tasks_held helpers, switched lifecycle.py to use Stop hook signal instead of JSONL idle heuristic for triggering companion sessions, and added test_permission_gate.py and test_task_approval_gate.py with 13 new passing tests. All 58 tests passing; commits pushed safely.

accomplishments.what: Ran autonomous safety-gate audit verifying unbuilt gates and race conditions in session-management core
Ran autonomous test-suite health audit verifying packaging readiness and test coverage
Confirmed session-management core (hooks, checkpoint, guard, state sync) is production-ready with 45 passing tests
Identified critical packaging blocker: homebrew Formula installs wrong package (root *.py only, not full askr/ package)
Documented pre-launch audit findings and blockers to roadmap.md and goals backlog
Discovered Discord webhook credential leaked in git history (rotated, requires git-filter-repo scrubbing)
Fixed Formula/askr.rb to install full askr/ package, create bin/askr entry point, install deps from requirements.txt
Implemented dangerous-permission task approval gate: added _peek_task_queue, _consume_approval_flag, _notify_tasks_held to session_start.py
Switched lifecycle.py from JSONL idle-time heuristic to Stop hook signal for triggering companion sessions
Added _signal_turn_stopped call in hooks/stop.py to mark turn completion for lifecycle polling
Verified all 45 tests still pass after lifecycle/stop/formula changes
Committed and pushed Formula/askr.rb, askr/hooks/stop.py, askr/session/lifecycle.py without blocking parallel session_start.py edits
Created test_permission_gate.py with 13 passing tests covering dangerous-permission detection, task queue peeking, approval flag consumption, and Discord notification flow
Created test_task_approval_gate.py with comprehensive coverage for task approval/discard CLI commands and held-task lifecycle
Updated roadmap.md Phase 5 status to reflect completed dangerous-permission task approval gate implementation
Verified full test suite: 58 tests passing (45 core + 13 new permission/approval gate tests)

in_progress.what: Architectural design for stateful retry mechanism that captures failure context (screenshots, error reasoning) to enable learning-based job resubmission instead of blind retry
Cut real git tag + GitHub release so Formula sha256/url are real, and create homebrew-askr tap repo

next_actions.action: Scrub Discord webhook credential from git history using git-filter-repo to remove leaked secret from all commits
next_actions.why: Security blocker: credential is exposed in git history and must be removed before any public release

next_actions.action: Cut real git tag (e.g. v0.1.0) and create GitHub release with accurate Formula sha256/url so homebrew-askr tap can reference real artifacts
next_actions.why: Formula currently has placeholder sha256/url; real release needed before tap repo can be published

next_actions.action: Create homebrew-askr tap repository with Formula pointing to real release artifacts and sha256
next_actions.why: Completes Phase 5 roadmap item and enables users to install askr via brew tap

next_actions.action: Extend pre_tool_use.py cross-repo boundary check to cover Bash tool calls, not just Write/Edit/MultiEdit
next_actions.why: Security gap: Bash commands can access files outside repo root without guard enforcement

next_actions.action: Add test coverage for pre_tool_use.py and guard_runner.py (implementation guard has zero tests despite being security-critical)
next_actions.why: Critical path lacks test coverage; must verify guard logic before production launch

next_actions.action: Fix PreCompact emergency handover to route through real LLM handover path instead of hardcoded boilerplate in checkpoint.py
next_actions.why: Emergency handovers currently use static template; should use full handover generation logic

next_actions.action: Update README.md to reflect Phase 3 (notifications) and Phase 3.5 (guard) as completed, not 'Coming Next'
next_actions.why: Documentation is stale; both phases are built and running

next_actions.action: Wire task_approval_pending notification type into Cursor extension.js for IDE popup rendering
next_actions.why: New notification type added but not wired to extension; also audit guard_warning rendering

decisions.decision: Use Stop hook signal instead of JSONL idle-time heuristic to trigger companion sessions
decisions.reason: Stop hook provides deterministic signal of turn completion; JSONL idle heuristic is unreliable and race-prone

decisions.decision: Hold queued tasks in dangerous-permission sessions instead of auto-injecting them
decisions.reason: Prevents unintended task execution in high-risk sessions; requires explicit approval via CLI or notification response

decisions.decision: Implement task approval gate as part of session_start.py flow, not as separate middleware
decisions.reason: Centralizes permission logic at session entry point; simplifies state management and approval flag consumption

relational_files.why: Switched from JSONL idle heuristic to Stop hook signal for companion session triggering
Added _signal_turn_stopped call to mark turn completion for lifecycle polling
Fixed to install full askr/ package, create bin/askr entry point, and install requirements.txt deps
Dangerous-permission task approval gate goal auto-completed by Stop-hook inference
Guard implementation; needs test coverage and Bash boundary check extension
Emergency handover path needs refactoring to use real LLM handover generation

blockers: Discord webhook credential leaked in git history — requires git-filter-repo scrubbing before public release
Formula sha256/url are placeholders — need real git tag + GitHub release before homebrew-askr tap can be published
pre_tool_use.py cross-repo boundary check does not cover Bash tool calls — security gap
completed_goals: Wire dangerous-permission detection into session start flow; hold queued tasks if session has dangerous permissions and no approval flag; notify via Discord and notification.json; add task approval/discard CLI commands
